message:
feat(mvcc/rcu): add E3.3 active-txn snapshot-table prototype with RCU publication

Landing the Track E3.3 active-transaction snapshot-table
prototype on top of the existing `rcu` helper module. This
provides an immutable, RCU-published image of the current
concurrent registry's active-session view — `(session_id,
snapshot_high)` pairs plus the GC horizon — so readers can
bind a single coherent view without taking the registry mutex
or observing a torn multi-field update.

The prototype is deliberately *additive*: it does not change
the current registry ownership model, it does not rewire the
hot validation path, and it does not replace
`ConcurrentRegistry::active_snapshot_highs`. It just makes the
active portion of that state publishable through the RCU
primitive so a future commit can swap validation readers over
to the snapshot-backed view.

## rcu.rs — new types

- `MAX_ACTIVE_TXN_SNAPSHOT_ENTRIES = 128` — representative
  capacity matching `begin_concurrent.rs::MAX_CONCURRENT_WRITERS`
  without coupling the RCU helper module to the transaction
  protocol.
- `ActiveTxnSnapshotEntry { session_id, snapshot_high }` — the
  immutable per-session entry published through the table.
- `ActiveTxnSnapshotImage { generation, gc_horizon, entries:
  SmallVec<[ActiveTxnSnapshotEntry; 8]> }` — owned
  point-in-time image returned by
  `RcuActiveTxnSnapshotTable::snapshot`. The generation counter
  lets readers detect "my image is older than the one another
  thread just produced" without reopening the RCU pair.
- `ActiveTxnSnapshotImage::len` / `is_empty` — convenience
  accessors.
- `ActiveTxnSnapshotSlot` — internal raw slot type holding
  per-entry atomic session-id / snapshot-high arrays, an
  atomic count, generation, and gc_horizon. Kept private so
  only the public `RcuActiveTxnSnapshotTable` can mutate it.
- `RcuActiveTxnSnapshotTable` — the public publication handle.
  Exposes `publish(entries, gc_horizon, qsbr_handle)` and
  `snapshot() -> ActiveTxnSnapshotImage`. Built on top of the
  existing `RcuPair` / `QsbrHandle` primitives so the same
  reclamation contract applies.

## lib.rs — re-exports

Extend the existing `pub use rcu::{...}` block to re-export
`ActiveTxnSnapshotEntry`, `ActiveTxnSnapshotImage`,
`MAX_ACTIVE_TXN_SNAPSHOT_ENTRIES`, and
`RcuActiveTxnSnapshotTable`. Alphabetical ordering is
preserved so rustfmt doesn't rewrite the block.

## begin_concurrent.rs — publication glue

`ConcurrentRegistry` gains two new methods, both additive:

- `active_snapshot_entries(&self) -> SmallVec<[...; 8]>` —
  walks the existing `active_snapshot_highs` map, builds one
  `ActiveTxnSnapshotEntry` per session, and sorts the result
  by `session_id` so the published image is deterministic.
- `publish_active_snapshot_prototype(&self, table,
  handle)` — thin wrapper that calls
  `active_snapshot_entries`, reads the current GC horizon via
  the existing `gc_horizon()` accessor, and hands both to
  `RcuActiveTxnSnapshotTable::publish` under the caller's
  QSBR handle.

Neither method changes the existing ownership model: the
`active_snapshot_highs` map remains the source of truth, and
the published image is a derived view that can be rebuilt
from scratch on demand. This is the same shape as the E3.2
RCU-for-schema-metadata story, adjusted for active-txn state.

## Why this lives in rcu.rs, not a new module

The RCU primitives already own the `RcuPair` / `RcuTriple` /
`QsbrRegistry` / `QsbrHandle` types and the per-epoch
metrics. Keeping the active-txn snapshot-table prototype in
the same file means:

- New code can reuse the existing `FSQLITE_RCU_RECLAIMED_TOTAL`
  / `FSQLITE_RCU_GRACE_PERIOD_*` counters without adding a
  parallel metrics surface.
- The publication and reclamation paths share the same
  acquire/release memory ordering rules, which are already
  documented at the top of `rcu.rs`.
- A future agent reading the E3.3 story has exactly one file
  to grep for snapshot-table semantics.
